claims.statement: s3kor's canonical repository is github.com/sethkor/s3kor.
claims.qualification: The identity was confirmed by cloning and pinning the repository at the reviewed commit.

claims.statement: s3kor is written in Go (module github.com/sethkor/s3kor, go 1.14).
claims.qualification: The go.mod module path and language version establish this; the inherited dossier already stated Go.

claims.statement: s3kor is licensed under GPL-3.0.
claims.qualification: The LICENSE file is GPLv3.

claims.statement: The reviewed s3kor build is module version v0.0.37, but because the go-install build omits goreleaser ldflags the binary self-reports its version as dev-local-version none unknown.
claims.qualification: The inherited dossier recorded the version as unknown. The running binary's panic stack embeds the github.com/sethkor/s3kor@v0.0.37 module path, while --version self-reports dev-local-version none unknown because go install injects no version ldflags; receipts also carry v0.0.37 as caller-supplied metadata.

claims.statement: The reviewed subject is s3kor pinned at commit 844fe3d7931fcca415c8b8a4e22f048886e6b82b, which corresponds to the latest release tag v0.0.37.
claims.qualification: The inherited dossier recorded the version as unknown; groundwork pins the reviewed subject at this commit, identified with release tag v0.0.37 (the latest release, tagged 2021-10-02), as recorded in the groundwork report.

claims.statement: Installing s3kor is trivial: goreleaser release binaries and go install both work.
claims.qualification: This narrows the inherited unqualified trivial claim to installation only; listing was untestable in this campaign (see credential-starved-listing-blocked).

claims.statement: Under the study's credential-starved anonymous environment, both s3kor listing modes were blocked, each producing no listing output, issuing zero S3 requests, and exiting with status 2.
claims.qualification: Both the list and list-versions capability receipts record the same blocked outcome; this settles that s3kor cannot list under CREDS=none, not any correctness verdict.

claims.statement: s3kor's listing client always uses the signing credential chain; neither of the two AnonymousCredentials uses (region detection and the S3-to-S3 copy download) is the listing client, so there is no unsigned listing path.
claims.qualification: Source establishes the absence of an unsigned listing path; the behavioral consequence is confirmed separately by credential-starved-listing-blocked.

claims.statement: Under the harness's credential-starved environment the failure manifests as a Go panic during AWS session construction (session.Must), before command dispatch, identical for both modes.
claims.qualification: The panic is scoped to this session-build-error condition (a web-identity token file set while the role ARN is empty); a bare empty-credential environment would instead fail at request time, but either way listing cannot run unsigned.

claims.statement: s3kor is a Go re-implementation of a subset of the aws s3 CLI (cp, rm, ls, sync), marketed as a drop-in replacement using multiple threads for fast parallel actions.
claims.qualification: The four subcommands are source-visible; the multiple-threads headline is transfer-side and does not describe listing.

claims.statement: cp and sync expose -c/--concurrent flags (defaults 30 and 20) while ls has no concurrency flag; the transfer worker code was not read, so worker spawning is documentation-attested only.
claims.qualification: The flag split is source-visible; the transfer-side parallel behavior is documentation-attested, not source-verified, and is out of listing scope.

claims.statement: The ls subcommand is s3kor's only listing surface and lists through a single serial ListObjectsV2Pages continuation-token chain with only Bucket and Prefix; per-page goroutines only format output and issue no LIST requests.
claims.qualification: Source confirms the serial mechanism, upgrading the inherited analogy-only hypothesis; it does not settle serial listing's cost at scale.

claims.statement: Whether serial listing is a performance weakness at scale is unestablished.
claims.qualification: Source settles the mechanism, not the cost at 10^6 to 10^8 keys; no benchmark or listing run exists and every listing was credential-blocked.

evidence.reason: Listing was credential-blocked and smoke is not a benchmark, so no scale timing exists.

claims.statement: s3kor's findings do not collapse into the s5cmd hypothesis: it has distinct findings including no unsigned listing path, a session-build panic, and a source-visible ls concurrency race.
claims.qualification: This corrects the inherited claim that the serial-listing inference would collapse s3kor into a one-line s5cmd corollary; the distinct findings are source-visible.

claims.statement: The version-listing flag is --all-versions, not --versions; it uses ListObjectVersionsPages and emits one line per version as versionId then key.
claims.qualification: This corrects the inherited --versions flag name; --versions does not exist, confirmed by source and live --help.

claims.statement: Whether list-versions has different performance characteristics than plain list is unestablished.
claims.qualification: The inherited quick-check sub-hypothesis is unrun; both listing modes were credential-blocked, so no comparison exists.

evidence.reason: Both listing modes were credential-blocked, so no perf comparison between the modes was run.

claims.statement: s3kor documents cross-account bucket-to-bucket copy via --dest-profile.
claims.qualification: This is a transfer feature, documentation-attested and source-visible as a flag; it was not run (out of listing scope and mutating).

claims.statement: The claim that s3kor has Go performance characteristics similar to s5cmd and rclone is unestablished.
claims.qualification: No performance was measured; smoke is not a benchmark and listing was blocked. The comparative claim is vague.

evidence.reason: No performance was measured; listing was blocked and smoke is not a benchmark.

claims.statement: The ls printer path carries a source-visible data race: per-page goroutines write a shared channel (non-deterministic cross-page order), and List starts the printer before reassigning the channel it ranges over and mis-sequences the WaitGroup, giving an abandoned-channel hang or a Done-before-Add panic.
claims.qualification: The race is source-visible; its runtime manifestation was not observed (see ls-race-runtime-behavior-unverified).

claims.statement: Whether the ls concurrency race manifests as a hang or a panic in a real run is unestablished.
claims.qualification: Every listing run was credential-blocked, so the race's runtime behavior was never observed.

evidence.reason: Every listing was credential-blocked, so no run exercised the race.

claims.statement: The upstream README documents a nonexistent --auto-region flag; the actual region auto-detection flag is --detect-region.
claims.qualification: This corrects the documented flag name; --auto-region does not exist, confirmed by source and live --help.

claims.statement: --verbose writes zap DevelopmentConfig logs to a temp file inside the container, not to stdout or stderr, so it provides no visible request trace.
claims.qualification: This is source-corroborated and was not separately observed at runtime.

claims.statement: Upstream is dormant: the last commit was 2022-06-14 (docs-only), the last release v0.0.37 was tagged 2021-10-02, and four issues are open.
claims.qualification: This is a dated third-party observation as of 2026-07-17, not a receipt.

claims.statement: Upstream ships neither a published container image nor a Dockerfile; distribution is a Homebrew tap and goreleaser release binaries, so the study builds from source.
claims.qualification: The distribution channels are source-visible; the absence of an image is partly an absence-of-evidence sweep (no sethkor/s3kor image found on Docker Hub or GHCR on 2026-07-17), not a cited document.

claims.statement: Listing is streaming but not back-pressured: the paginator callback spawns a goroutine per page and returns immediately, so the 50-slot channel caps blocked sends, not the count of blocked sender goroutines, and peak memory can grow with the number of in-flight pages.
claims.qualification: This is a source structural read, not a measured memory claim; the earlier bounded-by-backpressure reading was corrected in groundwork.

claims.statement: Whether the non-back-pressured design runs out of memory on million-object listings is unestablished.
claims.qualification: No scale run exists; the capability smoke recorded no memory number because the process panicked at startup.

evidence.reason: No scale listing ran; the capability smoke panicked at startup and captured no memory sample.

claims.statement: list-versions is manifest-comparable only on an unversioned bucket: ListObjectVersions returns every version and delete marker and normalize.sh drops the version id to a bare key, so on a versioned bucket it legitimately emits duplicate and delete-marker-only keys.
claims.qualification: The smoke bucket's versioning state is unrecorded and normalize.sh does not enforce it, so this precondition is unestablished; the mode was credential-blocked regardless.

claims.statement: s3kor supports linux amd64 and arm64 natively on every distribution channel, both the goreleaser release binaries and the pure-Go source build.
claims.qualification: The Windows architecture set is ambiguous between the README and .goreleaser.yml, but that is not load-bearing; the benchmark denominator is linux amd64/arm64, both native.

claims.statement: The capability smoke ran on arm64 natively, with image architecture arm64 on an arm64 host and no emulation.
claims.qualification: This is a fact about the smoke execution environment, recorded in run.meta, not a performance result.
